perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each may, at their discretion, contribute to the filing of such amendments, and information concerning him or it contained herein and therein. The responsibility for completeness and accuracy lies solely with the lead filer.
perturbation.explanation: By shifting responsibility for accuracy and completeness solely to the 'lead filer' and making participation in amendments discretionary, the agreement now undermines the individual accountability expected under securities regulations for accurate filings. This creates a loophole where individual signatories may claim non-responsibility.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 outlines the requirements for filing Schedule 13D and 13G, including who is deemed a beneficial owner and therefore responsible for filing. The alteration suggests that signatories may not be responsible for the accuracy of information, which contradicts the requirement that each beneficial owner is individually responsible for the information they are required to disclose.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.13d-1 Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. (a) Any person who, after acquiring directly or indirectly the beneficial ownership of any equity security of a class which is specified in paragraph (i)(1) of this section , is directly or indirectly the beneficial owner of more than five percent of the class shall, within five business days after the date of the acquisition, file with the Commission, a statement containing the information required by Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) . (b) (1) A person who would otherwise be obligated under paragraph (a) of this section to file a statement on Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) may, in lieu thereof, file with the Commission, a short-form statement on Schedule 13G (§ 240.13d-102), Provided, That: (i) Such person has acquired such securities in the ordinary course of the person's business and not with the purpose nor with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer , nor in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having such purpose or effect, including any transaction subject to § 240.13d-3(b) and (ii) Such person is: (A) A broker or dealer registered under section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78o ); (B) A bank as defined in section 3(a)(6) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (C) An insurance company as defined in section 3(a)(19) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (D) An investment company registered under section 8 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-8 ); (E) Any person registered as an investment adviser under Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ) or under the laws of any state; (F) An employee benefit plan as defined in Section 3(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security A
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts securities regulations by shifting responsibility for accuracy and completeness solely to the 'lead filer,' and making participation in amendments discretionary, undermining the individual accountability expected for accurate filings. The original text clearly states that each party is responsible for the accuracy of information concerning him or it, while the changed text implies that individual signatories may not be responsible for the accuracy of information, creating a potential loophole. According to the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation, this is a violation of 17 CFR § 240.13d-1, which requires each beneficial owner to be individually responsible for the information they are required to disclose. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing 17 CFR § 240.13d-1, outlines the requirements for filing Schedules 13D and 13G, and while it doesn't explicitly state individual responsibility for accuracy, the explanation provided asserts this expectation under securities regulations.

perturbation.original_text: This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Joint Filing Agreement may be presented for informational purposes; however, all obligations and filings are the sole responsibility of Yorkville Advisors Global, LP.
perturbation.explanation: This change transfers responsibility from all parties to a single entity, Yorkville Advisors Global, LP, thus exempting others from potential legal liability for disclosures. This contradicts SEC requirements for disclosure.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 78m(d), which is Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, stipulates reporting requirements for anyone acquiring more than 5% of a voting class of equity securities. This transfer of responsibility would potentially allow other parties to avoid their legal obligations to disclose ownership and intent as per this section.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 15 / CHAPTER 2B / § 78m Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 15 USC 78m : Periodical and other reports Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 15-COMMERCE AND TRADE CHAPTER 2B-SECURITIES EXCHANGES Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous References In Text Amendments Effective Date Construction Executive Documents §78m. Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The original text describes an agreement executed by multiple parties, whereas the changed text shifts the responsibility solely to Yorkville Advisors Global, LP. This contradicts the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which requires reporting from anyone acquiring more than 5% of voting equity. By assigning all filing responsibilities to one party, it could allow other parties to avoid disclosing ownership and intent, which is a direct violation of SEC rules. Snippet 1 mentions 15 USC 78m, which pertains to periodical and other reports and requires every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title to file information with the Commission, ensuring investor protection and fair dealing. The changed text undermines this by potentially shielding parties from these mandated reports, hence creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge the existence of a statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. However, the decision to file or amend said statement remains at the sole discretion of the filing agent, and agreement is not binding.
perturbation.explanation: This change undermines the binding nature of the agreement to file Schedule 13G by making the filing discretionary. The clause transforms the agreement from a binding obligation to a mere acknowledgement, which weakens the enforceability of SEC reporting requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1(k) governs the requirements for group filings under Schedule 13D and 13G, stipulating that agreements must be in writing and filed with the SEC. By stating the agreement is not binding, this modification directly contradicts the explicit requirement that a group filing agreement represents a binding obligation among the parties.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.13d-1 Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. (a) Any person who, after acquiring directly or indirectly the beneficial ownership of any equity security of a class which is specified in paragraph (i)(1) of this section , is directly or indirectly the beneficial owner of more than five percent of the class shall, within five business days after the date of the acquisition, file with the Commission, a statement containing the information required by Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) . (b) (1) A person who would otherwise be obligated under paragraph (a) of this section to file a statement on Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) may, in lieu thereof, file with the Commission, a short-form statement on Schedule 13G (§ 240.13d-102), Provided, That: (i) Such person has acquired such securities in the ordinary course of the person's business and not with the purpose nor with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer , nor in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having such purpose or effect, including any transaction subject to § 240.13d-3(b) and (ii) Such person is: (A) A broker or dealer registered under section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78o ); (B) A bank as defined in section 3(a)(6) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (C) An insurance company as defined in section 3(a)(19) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (D) An investment company registered under section 8 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-8 ); (E) Any person registered as an investment adviser under Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ) or under the laws of any state; (F) An employee benefit plan as defined in Section 3(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security A
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the legal requirement for a binding agreement regarding Schedule 13G filings. The original text establishes a clear agreement to file, while the changed text makes the filing discretionary, undermining the enforceability of SEC reporting requirements. The Law Explanation specifies that 17 CFR § 240.13d-1(k) requires such agreements to be binding. Although the provided scraped snippets do not explicitly state this requirement, the Law Explanation's reference to a specific regulation supports the assessment that the change introduces a contradiction by removing a legally mandated obligation.
